description: A middle school team is entering the American Rocketry Challenge (TARC). Adapt the standard model rocket design in simple_model_rocket.ork to carry a fragile payload to a target altitude of 259.0 meters (± 3.0 meters) with an egg-safe descent rate. The rocket must be upscaled to a body tube diameter of at least 46 mm. Include two mass components in the forward section: 'Egg' (65 g) and 'Altimeter' (15 g). Add a third mass component named 'Tuning Ballast' and adjust its mass and the motor selection to achieve the target apogee. Size the parachute to ensure a ground hit velocity of 4.5 m/s or less. Save the design to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/tarc_competition_design.ork. Document the final body tube diameter, motor selected, tuning ballast mass, final simulated apogee, and ground hit velocity in a report at /home/ga/Documents/exports/tarc_engineering_notebook.md.
